risks.description: Similar belief/coordination substrate ships before Jun 13
risks.mitigation: Publish benchmarks and the 'belief-as-a-message' category flag by end of Phase D. Stake out the differentiator early via docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

risks.description: LLM-judge merge is slow or flaky on stage
risks.mitigation: LLM-judge is opt-in; default merge path is deterministic (LWW + OR-set).

risks.description: Embedding costs spike during the demo
risks.mitigation: Local bge-small fallback; aggressive cache; batched embeddings. Feature-flag via WEAVORY_EMBEDDING=local.

risks.description: weavory.ai trademark or npm scope clash
risks.mitigation: Week-1 npm-scope + USPTO/WIPO name check. Fallback names: Koine, Plexus.

risks.description: NANDA AgentFacts schema drifts during build
risks.mitigation: Pin to a specific AgentFacts commit; document the pin in docs/NANDA.md; track upstream weekly.

risks.description: Phase-1 judge test fails on stage
risks.mitigation: Three end-to-end dress-rehearsals on fresh VMs before May 7. Gate 7 script is the non-negotiable blocker.

risks.description: Federation (libp2p) destabilizes the demo
risks.mitigation: Feature-flagged (WEAVORY_FEDERATION=1), off by default, Phase G only.

risks.description: Runtime snapshot writes add perceptible latency under burst ops
risks.mitigation: 50ms debounce + atomic tmp+rename + single-writer invariant. In-memory state updates synchronously; only disk writes are debounced.

risks.description: LLM-judge merge introduces external API dependency mid-session (Phase G.2)
risks.mitigation: Opt-in behind WEAVORY_LLM_JUDGE=1. Default merge is deterministic (LWW + OR-set + consensus vote).

risks.description: Phase-G features creep outside the five-tool public API
risks.mitigation: Every Phase-G change must leave src/mcp/server.ts tool registration untouched; Gate 2 script enforces the five-tool whitelist on every CI run.

risks.description: Phase-G work regresses the Phase-1 judge path (Gate 7)
risks.mitigation: Every sub-phase re-runs full Vitest suite and local gate scripts 1-5 before committing; CI re-runs fresh-machine workflow including Gate 7 on every push.

risks.description: DuckDB native binary fails on Gate 6 CI matrix arch (ABI mismatch, missing prebuilt)
risks.mitigation: Three-layer defense in place: (1) @duckdb/node-api is optionalDependencies so pnpm install never fails on binary-resolve errors; (2) persist_duckdb.ts uses dynamic `await import()` inside the factory; (3) openPersistentStore catches any load failure and transparently falls back to JSONL with one stderr warning. Tested locally and encoded in unit tests (persist_duckdb.test.ts fallback assertion).

risks.description: DuckDB fire-and-forget write queue loses last few ms of writes on SIGKILL
risks.mitigation: Documented in docs/DEPLOYMENT.md as the documented durability tradeoff for the DuckDB backend. Ordering is still strict via single-threaded write queue. JSONL backend (default) has no such risk — fs.appendFileSync is synchronously durable.

risks.description: Audit chain tampered directly on disk under WEAVORY_PERSIST=1
risks.mitigation: On every `weavory start`, the rehydrated audit chain is re-verified. Broken chain → exit code 3 with bad_index + reason. Documented response in docs/RUNBOOK.md section 'Chain-broken on restart'. This is a feature (tamper detection), not a bug.

risks.description: Malformed WEAVORY_POLICY_FILE silently disables policy enforcement
risks.mitigation: loadPolicy throws on any IO / JSON / schema error; CLI exits code 4 with operator-actionable message. Policy is never silently degraded to no-policy.

risks.description: believe() had no default payload size cap when WEAVORY_POLICY_FILE was unset → disk-fill / memory-bloat DoS
risks.mitigation: Phase J.P1-4 shipped: DEFAULT_MAX_OBJECT_BYTES=1 MiB enforced in ops.believe when no policy is attached. Throws OversizedPayloadError with observed_bytes + limit_bytes. Policy file may raise the cap up to 16 MiB. Unit tests cover accept/reject/policy-override paths.

risks.description: No cap on total subscriptions per EngineState → a misbehaving agent could register unbounded subscriptions and exhaust memory
risks.mitigation: Phase J.P1-4 shipped: EngineState.subscriptionsCap defaults to 10 000 (WEAVORY_MAX_SUBSCRIPTIONS env override). ops.subscribe throws SubscriptionLimitError when the cap is reached; size unchanged. parseSubscriptionsCap validates env input and falls back to the default with a warning on garbage values.

risks.description: loadIncident() parsed raw JSON with only a schema_version string check before handing to rehydrateState → malformed or truncated files surfaced as cryptic TypeErrors mid-rehydrate
risks.mitigation: Phase J.P1-4 shipped: Zod LoadIncidentShape validates outer record fields (incident_id, exported_at, audit{length,verify,entries}, beliefs{total,live,quarantined,tombstoned,records}) before rehydrate. Structured error messages for missing file, invalid JSON, wrong schema_version, and shape violation.

risks.description: WEAVORY_DATA_DIR and WEAVORY_POLICY_FILE paths are not symlink-hardened — an attacker with env + filesystem access could redirect I/O via symlinks
risks.mitigation: Operator-owned trust boundary: the process environment is the operator's responsibility. Weavory logs the resolved absolute path once at startup so operators can see exactly what the server will touch. Full hardening (path prefix allow-list, symlink refusal) deferred as a Phase-2 concern. Documented in docs/SECURITY.md.

risks.description: exportIncident dumps full belief `object` into JSON under ops/data/incidents/ — incident file is as sensitive as the source beliefs
risks.mitigation: Incidents directory is gitignored by default; filesystem permissions govern access. Recommended operator control is to set WEAVORY_POLICY_FILE with predicate_deny rules so sensitive predicates never enter the store. Redaction during export is Phase-2. Documented in docs/SECURITY.md.

risks.description: Stderr logs include absolute WEAVORY_DATA_DIR and WEAVORY_POLICY_FILE paths — may leak deployment topology
risks.mitigation: No code change. Seeds (signer_seed) are never logged. Operators can route stderr through their logging pipeline's redaction. Documented in docs/SECURITY.md.
